summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a rare 1968 gig packed with jazzy gems – standout Clementine, New Potato, Born Cross‑Eyed and Spanish Jam.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear soundboard source that lets every instrument shine, delivering an audio feast for fans of the pre‑1970 Dead. Musically the show is a treasure trove of rarities – the first Clementine, a superb New Potato, the early Born Cross‑Eyed and a killer Spanish Jam – all performed with tight transitions and a jazzy, Coltrane‑inflected vibe.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are five‑star, praising both the superb soundboard quality and the exceptional, rarity‑filled performance; weightings give an overall near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audio is described as crisp, clear and able to hear every instrument; no technical flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers rave about the smooth, jazzy transitions and the overall flow, calling the set a rare audio feast.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for spacious, deliberate guitar on New Potato and strong vocal delivery on Clementine.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for writing Clementine, his Coltrane‑inspired bass approach, and overall huge contribution to the jazzy vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen shines on keyboards, delivering top‑form playing throughout the jazzy sections.
